Software Review

Buildertrend Review (2026)

The market leader for residential construction software. Here's what it actually delivers.

⭐ Rating: 4.5/5 — Best for residential builders and remodelers

Buildertrend is the dominant construction project management platform for residential builders and remodeling contractors. With over 1 million users and a feature set that covers the full project lifecycle, it's become the default choice for custom homebuilders, production builders, and full-service remodelers.

But at $499–$1,099/month, it's a significant investment. This review gives you the real picture: what it does well, where it falls short, and who should (and shouldn't) use it.

Pricing

Plan Monthly Price Users Key Features Added
Essential $499/mo Unlimited Core PM, scheduling, client portal, daily logs, selections
Advanced $799/mo Unlimited Advanced reporting, custom templates, integrations
Complete $1,099/mo Unlimited Lead management, bidding, full financial suite

Important: All plans include unlimited users. This matters — there are no per-seat charges for adding your project managers, office staff, subs, and clients to the platform. For a team of 10–20, this is a genuine cost advantage over per-seat competitors.

Buildertrend does not offer a free trial in the traditional sense — they demo the software with you and let you evaluate before committing. Annual plans save approximately 15% vs. month-to-month.

Core features

Project scheduling

Buildertrend uses a Gantt-style scheduling view with task dependencies and automated delay notification. When one task gets pushed, the downstream tasks in the schedule update accordingly. Subs can be assigned to specific tasks and receive automated notifications when their work window is approaching.

This feature alone is worth significant time savings for builders managing multiple simultaneous projects.

Client portal

The owner-facing portal is Buildertrend's most-praised feature. Clients log in to see the project schedule, approve selections, view progress photos, sign change orders, and make payments — all without calling you. For custom homebuilders dealing with emotionally invested clients, the transparency the portal provides reduces friction dramatically.

Selections management

Critical for homebuilders: track every owner selection (flooring type, cabinet hardware, light fixtures, etc.) with allowance tracking and approval workflows. When an owner claims they never chose that tile, you have a timestamped approval record to reference.

Change order management

Buildertrend's change order workflow covers pricing, markup, client presentation, digital signature collection, and automatic budget update. The process that used to require an email chain, a PDF, a separate signature request, and a manual budget update now happens in one workflow.

Financial tools

Budget tracking, draw schedules, invoicing, and payment collection are all included. The integration with QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Desktop syncs bills, invoices, and payments to reduce double data entry. The integration works well but isn't perfect — plan for some manual reconciliation.

Daily logs and progress photos

Field crews can log weather, crew count, and progress notes from the mobile app. Progress photos are timestamped, tagged to the project, and visible to clients via the portal. This documentation is increasingly important for disputes and insurance purposes.

Subcontractor management

Send bid requests to your sub list, track responses, issue purchase orders, and manage lien waivers — all within Buildertrend. Subs get a portal to access their schedule, documents, and task assignments without seeing your full financial data.

What Buildertrend does well

  • Comprehensive feature coverage for residential construction
  • Excellent client portal that differentiates you from competitors
  • Unlimited users at a flat monthly price
  • Strong scheduling with dependency tracking
  • Genuine selections management built for homebuilders
  • Solid QuickBooks integration (two-way sync)
  • Large user community and training resources (Buildertrend University)

Where Buildertrend falls short

  • Price — $499/mo is real money for small builders. If you're doing under $750K/year in revenue, it's hard to justify.
  • Implementation time — Getting the platform fully set up takes 60–90 days minimum. There's a real learning curve and it takes time to build your templates and workflows.
  • Mobile app — Better than it used to be, but field crews still find the desktop experience more complete. The app is functional but not best-in-class.
  • Commercial projects — Buildertrend isn't built for commercial. If you have a commercial GC side of your business, you'll outgrow it quickly.
  • Customer support — Support quality is inconsistent based on user reports. Onboarding support is strong; ongoing support varies.
  • Reporting — Standard reports are solid; custom reporting requires the Advanced plan or manual export to Excel.

Who Buildertrend is for

  • Custom homebuilders doing 3–50 homes per year
  • Remodeling contractors doing $1M+ in annual revenue
  • Design-build firms where client communication is a competitive differentiator
  • Builders who want to consolidate from multiple tools into one platform

Who should look elsewhere

  • Solo contractors or very small operations under $500K/year (look at JobTread)
  • Design-build remodelers with strong design emphasis (consider Houzz Pro)
  • Commercial GCs (consider Procore)
  • Trade contractors (HVAC, plumbing, electrical — see TradeSoftwareGuide.com for field service software)

Buildertrend — If you're a residential builder or remodeler ready to consolidate your project management, Buildertrend is the market standard. Request a demo to see the platform in context of your specific business.

Visit Buildertrend →

Alternatives to consider

  • JobTread ($99–$499/mo) — If Buildertrend's price is the issue, JobTread is the most credible alternative with comparable core features at lower cost.
  • Houzz Pro ($65–$399/mo) — For design-build remodelers where design and lead generation matter.
  • Procore (custom pricing) — For commercial GCs who've outgrown residential platforms.

Frequently asked questions

Does Buildertrend offer a free trial?

Not a traditional self-service free trial. Buildertrend demos the software with you and walks through how it fits your business. This is intentional — the platform has depth that's hard to evaluate in a 14-day free trial without guidance.

How long does Buildertrend implementation take?

Most users report 2–3 months to get fully functional. The onboarding includes setup calls and access to Buildertrend University. Budget time for building your cost templates, schedule templates, and training your team.

Can I cancel Buildertrend anytime?

Month-to-month contracts can be cancelled with notice. Annual contracts are discounted but typically require full-year commitment. Verify cancellation terms when you sign up.

Is there a Buildertrend mobile app?

Yes — iOS and Android apps are available. The mobile experience covers daily logs, progress photos, schedules, and communication. It's functional but the desktop interface remains more complete for administrative tasks.

Affiliate disclosure: Links to Buildertrend are affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you sign up through our link. This review is based on independent research and does not reflect paid placement. Full disclosure →
